free_text: Spallation of nickel and copper isotopes by protons
with energy 3.65 GeV
English transl.
. All targets were collected in one stack.
The targets were separated by Mylar foils of thickness
17.5 mg/cm2. This made it unnecessary to adopt any
special measures against the effect of secondary
scattering in the neighboring targets.
Metallic foils of Ni-58,Ni-60,Ni-62 and oxide powder
Cu-63 and Cu-65,effective thickness was 17-32 mg/cm**2,
purity of all targets was better than 99%.
Ge(Li) detector of 50 cm3 and multi-channel
analyzer with 4096 channels
Tbl.1 from YF,53,722,1991 (same in SNP,53,451)
.The uncertainty is reported by authors.
This uncertainty includes efficiency uncertainty and
statistical uncertainty.
The 63Cu and 65Cu targets contained aluminum and epoxy
resin, and therefore for these targets it was
impossible to determine the cross sections for
production of radioactive nuclides with mass
lower than 27. (from the article)
. last checking has been done.
SD:Updated to new date formats,lower case.
Corrected according to last EXFOR rules and Dict.
Ref. on English transl. (SNP,53,451,1991) added.
*By compiler*
1.  the production of Mn-52 due to radioactive decay of
Fe-52 is possible too.
2.  The production of Cr-51 due to radioactive decay of
Mn-51 is possible too.
3.  The production of Sc-47 (half-life 3.35 d) due to
radioactive decay of Ca-47 (half-life 4.54 d) is
possible too. The authors did not comment this
possibility.
See comment item 1.
see comment item 2.
see comment item 3.
The production due to isomer state is included.
The production due to isomer state decay is
included probably.
The cross section for long life isomer of Fe-52
has been measured probably.
The production of Mn-52 due to radioactive decay
of short life isomer of Fe-52 (half life 46 sec) is
possible too.
The production of Cr-51 due to radioactive decay
of Mn-51 (half-life 46 min) is not excluded.
